title: Ted And Mary

jokes.text: You gonna do that little wiggle step again? That was cute.
jokes.setup: Larry is about to bowl for the team
jokes.punchline: Ted mockingly comments on Larry's bowling form with condescending cuteness
jokes.explanation: Ted's condescending tone while trying to be encouraging establishes his character as obliviously irritating

jokes.text: No!
jokes.setup: Larry needs a spare to win, everyone's watching
jokes.punchline: Larry's devastated reaction to missing the spare
jokes.explanation: Simple but effective moment of Larry's competitive disappointment

jokes.text: I don't know if I can spend another evening with these people.
jokes.setup: Ted and Mary just invited them to a Paul Simon concert
jokes.punchline: Larry immediately complains about spending time with them despite accepting the invitation
jokes.explanation: Perfect Larry David hypocrisy - accepts social invitation while immediately regretting it

jokes.text: Those aren't mine. No, they gotta be. They're not my shoes.
jokes.setup: Getting shoes back at bowling alley
jokes.punchline: Employee insists wrong shoes must be Larry's despite Larry knowing they're not his
jokes.explanation: Sets up the shoe mix-up plot with classic service industry stubbornness

jokes.text: What kind of guy would take somebody else's shoes? A guy that has these shoes.
jokes.setup: Larry asking about the type of person who'd steal shoes
jokes.punchline: Employee's matter-of-fact response about the shoe thief's footwear situation
jokes.explanation: Brilliant circular logic delivered deadpan - very quotable

jokes.text: Everything's heaven with him. The piece of gum he had, 'Oh, this is heaven.' Had to taste a chocolate bar. 'I'm in heaven.' The parking space is 'heaven.' It's all 'heaven.'
jokes.setup: Larry explaining why Ted is strange
jokes.punchline: Escalating list of mundane things Ted calls 'heaven'
jokes.explanation: Excellent character observation with escalating rhythm, very quotable Ted Danson critique

jokes.text: He asked lots of questions about my personal hygiene, too. I'm not gonna even tell you what they were... but really personal.
jokes.setup: Larry is listing Ted's odd behaviors
jokes.punchline: The implication that Ted asked inappropriate hygiene questions
jokes.explanation: Creates uncomfortable humor through implication and Larry's discomfort

jokes.text: It must be hell living with Ted Danson. That's got to be hard.
jokes.setup: Discussing Ted's overly positive attitude
jokes.punchline: Larry uses 'hell' to describe living with someone who calls everything 'heaven'
jokes.explanation: Perfect wordplay callback to the 'heaven' complaint, plus meta-humor about celebrity guest

jokes.text: Why couldn't you be friends with her? Because she's a woman.
jokes.setup: Cheryl asks why Larry can't be friends with Mary
jokes.punchline: Larry's matter-of-fact explanation about gender barriers
jokes.explanation: Classic Larry social observation, though fairly predictable

jokes.text: If we're gonna be making friends with them... I'd just as soon be friends with her. You can be friends with him. And you could get the personal hygiene questions, okay?
jokes.setup: Larry proposing they split up friendship duties
jokes.punchline: Passing off Ted's weird personal questions to Cheryl
jokes.explanation: Good callback to earlier mention of Ted's inappropriate questions

jokes.text: Heaven, it was heaven.
jokes.setup: Larry talking to Mary on the phone about bowling
jokes.punchline: Larry ironically using Ted's signature phrase
jokes.explanation: Larry mockingly adopting Ted's language, good callback timing

jokes.text: I don't think.... They don't let me into certain states, actually. Why not? I'm incorrigible.
jokes.setup: Mary's mother invites Larry to Arkansas
jokes.punchline: Larry's absurd claim about being banned from states
jokes.explanation: Absurd Larry David self-aggrandizement about his troublemaking

jokes.text: Barneys salespeople, they're practically psychic.
jokes.setup: Salesperson correctly guesses Larry's jacket size
jokes.punchline: Over-the-top claim about Barneys staff abilities
jokes.explanation: Mild retail humor about pushy salespeople

jokes.text: Twins.
jokes.setup: Larry and Mary both buying the same jacket
jokes.punchline: Mary's enthusiastic comment about their matching outfits
jokes.explanation: Sets up future awkwardness about matching clothes

jokes.text: Nothing gives me more pleasure than cracking an egg. Nothing. I'll make you some omelets. I will flip you out.
jokes.setup: Larry claiming he loves to cook
jokes.punchline: Absurdly passionate description of egg-cracking
jokes.explanation: Larry's ridiculous overstatement about mundane cooking tasks

jokes.text: That is so sexy. Mary! I'm sorry. We're friends, I can say that. No big deal. I know, I'm sorry. I'm just very old-fashioned about that kind of.... No, Larry, honey, that's my water.
jokes.setup: Mary calls Larry's cooking sexy
jokes.punchline: Awkward backtracking followed by Larry drinking from her glass
jokes.explanation: Multi-layered awkwardness that sets up later consequences

jokes.text: That's bizarre, don't you think? Why? I've never been able to get you to go shopping before.
jokes.setup: Larry went shopping with Mary
jokes.punchline: Cheryl pointing out Larry's shopping hypocrisy
jokes.explanation: Good marital dynamics humor about Larry's selective enthusiasm

jokes.text: So, a shopping spree with the girls, Larry?
jokes.setup: Ted seeing Larry with shopping bags
jokes.punchline: Ted's emasculating comment about Larry's shopping trip
jokes.explanation: Ted's casual emasculation of Larry's shopping enthusiasm

jokes.text: Maybe you just figured wrong.
jokes.setup: Ted suggesting Larry isn't a sports guy
jokes.punchline: Larry's simple but pointed comeback
jokes.explanation: Clean, economical comeback to Ted's assumption

jokes.text: Call Mary first, figure out what to wear.
jokes.setup: Ted is saying goodbye about the Paul Simon concert
jokes.punchline: His suggestion that Larry coordinate outfits with Mary
jokes.explanation: Ted's sly dig at Larry's fashion coordination with his wife

jokes.text: How can they be your shoes? How can they be? Because that guy gave them to you by mistake the other day.
jokes.setup: Confronting the guy wearing Larry's shoes
jokes.punchline: Guy's bewildered response to Larry's obvious logic
jokes.explanation: Good cringe comedy about obvious social transgression

jokes.text: What's weird is that you'd take shoes that don't belong to you and put them on.
jokes.setup: Discussing what's weird about the shoe situation
jokes.punchline: Larry's logical breakdown of the real problem
jokes.explanation: Classic Larry logic applied to social transgression

jokes.text: That would be, that'd be kind of weird. No, that's gonna be weird for you now, after I get the shoes back.
jokes.setup: Discussion of what would be weird
jokes.punchline: Larry predicting the guy's future weirdness
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of the absurd shoe logic

jokes.text: Or even weirder that you left without even your shoes.
jokes.setup: Larry already called the guy weird
jokes.punchline: Larry doubling down with an even stranger accusation
jokes.explanation: Perfect Larry escalation - making a reasonable point in the most antagonistic way possible

jokes.text: I used desalter liquid with a scrub brush... and then I used a deodorizer spray. They smell like new shoes.
jokes.setup: Larry getting his shoes cleaned after stranger wore them
jokes.punchline: Elaborate cleaning process described in detail
jokes.explanation: Good cringe about the necessity of shoe fumigation

jokes.text: You asked me to waive the deposit and I did that for you. You looked like an honest person and I trusted your face.
jokes.setup: Larry canceling shoe order
jokes.punchline: Salesperson's manipulative guilt trip
jokes.explanation: Good retail manipulation tactics humor

jokes.text: I am not a shoe whore.
jokes.setup: Larry offering to pay commission
jokes.punchline: Salesperson's indignant response
jokes.explanation: Unexpectedly specific and quotable indignation from salesperson

jokes.text: A lie is a gesture, it's a courtesy. It's a little respect. This is very disrespectful.
jokes.setup: Ted and Mary haven't called about the concert
jokes.punchline: Larry's philosophy on polite lying
jokes.explanation: Brilliant Larry David social philosophy - perfectly crafted and quotable

jokes.text: And I went.... Like that.
jokes.setup: Larry explaining drinking from Mary's mother's glass
jokes.punchline: Larry's dismissive gesture about the incident
jokes.explanation: Good callback moment revealing why they weren't invited

jokes.text: I can't believe you're even acting like you don't know why we're not invited.
jokes.setup: Larry's obliviousness about the water glass incident
jokes.punchline: Cheryl's exasperated reaction to Larry's cluelessness
jokes.explanation: Good straight-man reaction to Larry's social blindness

jokes.text: I can't believe you're even acting like you don't know why we're not invited.
jokes.setup: Larry acts confused about why they weren't invited
jokes.punchline: Cheryl's exasperated response to his obliviousness
jokes.explanation: Cheryl's frustration with Larry's self-deception

jokes.text: You know, it's kind of half-jacket, half-shirt, half-man, half-beast.
jokes.setup: Larry returning the jacket to Barneys
jokes.punchline: Escalating description of the jacket's confusing nature
jokes.explanation: Good escalation from practical to mythological description

jokes.text: You know, it's kind of half-jacket, half-shirt, half-man, half-beast.
jokes.setup: Larry is returning the jacket to Barneys
jokes.punchline: His escalating description of the garment
jokes.explanation: Brilliant escalation from describing clothing to mythological creatures

jokes.text: I know your little game, and I'm not playing it.
jokes.setup: Larry trying to return the jacket
jokes.punchline: Salesperson's paranoid accusation
jokes.explanation: Good callback to earlier shoe incident creating paranoia

jokes.text: Sunday night? We thought it was last night. No, we went last night. He's playing three nights, Friday, Saturday, Sunday.
jokes.setup: The concert mix-up is revealed
jokes.punchline: They were invited to a different night all along
jokes.explanation: Good plot reversal that recontextualizes their paranoia

jokes.text: Bill and Hillary flew in this morning... and this evening was the only time they could all get together.
jokes.setup: Explaining where Ted and Mary are
jokes.punchline: Casual name-dropping of the Clintons
jokes.explanation: Callback to Mary's Clinton connections, shows Larry was displaced by celebrities

jokes.text: I guess it's you and me, huh? Guess so.
jokes.setup: Larry alone at concert with Mary's elderly mother
jokes.punchline: Awkward resignation to their pairing
jokes.explanation: Perfect ending callback - Larry stuck with the woman whose water he drank

tmdb_overview: Larry and Cheryl's fun-filled bowling date with Ted Danson and Mary Steenburgen ends with Larry's shoes missing.
